input.title: E-Commerce Platform
input.description: This document describes the Software Requirements Specification (SRS) for an E-Commerce platform, a web-based shopping platform for buying and selling products online

user_classes.Customer: End-users who interact with the product catalog, shopping cart, and checkout.
user_classes.Admin: Users with the ability to manage products, orders, and users.

definitions.Customer: A user who browses and purchases products.
definitions.Admin: A user with administrative privileges for managing the platform.

constraints: No mobile application development is included in the scope
Integration with third-party marketplaces and cryptocurrency payment support is out of scope

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based application accessible via modern browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari).
operating_environment.backend: Node.js with Next.js framework, Prisma ORM for database interactions, and MongoDB for data storage.

user_interface: Web-based interface with responsive design for both desktop and mobile screen sizes
hardware_interfaces: No specific hardware requirements; the system will be deployed on cloud infrastructure
software_interfaces: The backend will interact with MongoDB via Prisma ORM for data storage
communication_interfaces: The system will communicate with email servers to send confirmation emails

FR-01.title: User Login
requirements: Two input fields for email and password
The email must be validated for the correct format
The password field should have a show/hide toggle

FR-02.title: User Registration
requirements: Registration form with name, email, and password fields
Password must be at least 8 characters with one uppercase and one number
System shall send verification email after registration
Duplicate email addresses shall not be allowed

FR-03.title: Product Catalog
requirements: Display all products with name, image, price, and category
Users can filter products by category and price range
Users can sort products by price, name, and newest
Pagination with 20 products per page

FR-04.title: Shopping Cart
requirements: Users can add products to cart with quantity selection
Users can update item quantities in cart
Users can remove items from cart
Cart displays subtotal, tax, and grand total
Cart persists across sessions for logged-in users

FR-05.title: Order Placement
requirements: Users can place orders from cart with delivery address
System validates stock availability before order confirmation
Order confirmation page shows order summary
System generates unique order ID

NFR-01.title: Performance
requirements: The system shall load pages in under 2 seconds
API response time shall be under 500ms

NFR-02.title: Security
requirements: All passwords must be hashed using bcrypt
SSL encryption for all client-server communication
JWT tokens for authentication with 24-hour expiry

system_attributes.reliability: The system should be available 99.9% of the time
system_attributes.scalability: The platform should be able to scale horizontally to support increased traffic during peak seasons
system_attributes.security: All passwords should be hashed and stored securely; SSL encryption must be used for all communications between the client and server
system_attributes.maintainability: The platform should have a modular architecture, making it easy to add new features or update existing ones

FR-01.title: Plan User Login Workflow and Contracts
FR-01.description: Review the SRS requirement bullets for user login and break them into executable API, UI, data, security, and reporting subtasks. Define the workflow states, user roles, field-level validations, request/response contracts, and failure cases before implementation starts. Use these requirement drivers as the baseline: Two input fields for email and password; The email must be validated for the correct format; The password field should have a show/hide toggle.
acceptance_criteria: The end-to-end workflow for user login is documented from trigger to completion
Validation rules, role restrictions, and error scenarios for user login are explicitly defined
Implementation subtasks for user login can be picked up independently by engineering

FR-01.title: Implement User Login Backend Services
FR-01.description: Build the backend services for user login on Next.js, Prisma, MongoDB. Create route handlers or service endpoints, model the core business operations, enforce validation and authorization rules, persist workflow state changes, and add structured error handling and audit logging. Make sure the implementation covers these behaviors: Two input fields for email and password; The email must be validated for the correct format; The password field should have a show/hide toggle.
acceptance_criteria: Core user login business rules are implemented for E-Commerce Platform
Validation, authorization, and error handling are covered for user login
API/service behavior for user login is testable and documented for integration

FR-01.title: Implement User Login Frontend Flow
FR-01.description: Build the frontend flow for user login with the exact forms, tables, filters, wizards, status indicators, and action buttons required by the SRS. Connect the UI to the backend contract, handle optimistic updates or refresh flows where needed, and provide user-facing validation, permissions feedback, loading states, and recovery states.
acceptance_criteria: The user login user flow is accessible and responsive across supported browsers
Forms, filters, tables, and feedback states for user login behave correctly
The user login UI is fully integrated with backend APIs and handles loading and error states

FR-01.title: Integrate External Dependencies for User Login
FR-01.description: Implement the external integration points used by user login, such as third-party APIs, SSO, messaging gateways, payment rails, import/export pipelines, or bank feeds. Add retry-safe behavior, timeout handling, observability, and fallback behavior for downstream failures.
acceptance_criteria: External dependencies used by user login are connected through stable integration layers
Timeout, retry, and failure handling for user login integrations are implemented and testable
Secrets, credentials, and integration logs for user login are handled securely

FR-01.title: Write User Login Automated and End-to-End Tests
FR-01.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for user login. Cover valid flows, invalid inputs, permission failures, state transitions, data integrity checks, and regression-prone edge cases pulled from the SRS. Add fixtures or mocks that make the feature verifiable in CI and local development.
acceptance_criteria: Happy path and failure cases for user login are covered by automated tests
Permission checks and validation rules for user login are verified
Regression coverage exists for the most critical user login scenarios
